Kimi K2.5 is a MoE architecture foundation model with exceptional code and Agent capabilities. We use Claude Code, VS Code & Cline/RooCode as examples to illustrate how to use the Kimi K2.5 series models.

## [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#usage-notes)  Usage Notes

When using large models for code generation, due to the randomness and complexity of the model, multiple attempts may be required to generate code that meets expectations. Programming tools will automatically perform multiple rounds of retries and calls, which may lead to rapid token usage growth. To better control costs and improve the user experience, we recommend you pay attention to the following points:

- **Budget Control**
  - **Set Daily Spending Limit**: Before use, please go to [Kimi Open Platform Project Settings](https://platform.kimi.ai/console/projects/settings) to configure the “Project Daily Spending Budget”. Once the budget limit is reached, the system will automatically reject all API requests under this project (Note: Due to billing delays, the limit may take about 10 minutes to take effect). For setup instructions, please see [Organization Management Best Practices](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/org-best-practice)
  - **Balance Alert Reminder**: It is recommended to enable the account balance reminder function. When the account balance falls below the preset amount (default ¥20), the system will notify you via SMS to recharge in a timely manner.
- **Usage Recommendations**
  - **Continuous Monitoring**: It is recommended to keep monitoring while the programming software is running, and handle abnormal situations promptly to avoid unnecessary resource consumption due to infinite loops or excessive retries.
  - **Model Selection**: If response speed is not a high priority, you can choose to use the `kimi-k2.5` model.

## [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#k2-vendor-verifier)  K2 Vendor Verifier

Kimi models have always focused on agentic loops, and the reliability of tool calls is crucial. To this end, we launched [K2 Vendor Verifier (K2VV)](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/K2-Vendor-Verifier) to evaluate the quality of Kimi API tool calls from different vendors, helping you intuitively compare the accuracy differences among various vendors.**Latest Update**: K2VV has been expanded to 12 vendors, and more test data has been open-sourced. Welcome to provide feedback on the test metrics you care about [here](https://github.com/MoonshotAI/K2-Vendor-Verifier/issues/9).

## [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#using-kimi-k2-5-model-in-claude-code)  Using kimi k2.5 model in Claude Code

### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#install-claude-code)  Install Claude Code

If you have already installed Claude Code, you can skip this step

#### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#macos-and-linux)  MacOS and Linux

```
# Install nodejs on MacOS and Linux
curl -fsSL https://fnm.vercel.app/install | bash

# Open a new terminal to make fnm take effect
fnm install 24.3.0
fnm default 24.3.0
fnm use 24.3.0

# Install claude-code
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com

# Initialize configuration
node --eval "
    const homeDir = os.homedir();
    const filePath = path.join(homeDir, '.claude.json');
    if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
        const content = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8'));
        fs.writeFileSync(filePath,JSON.stringify({ ...content, hasCompletedOnboarding: true }, 2), 'utf-8');
    } else {
        fs.writeFileSync(filePath,JSON.stringify({ hasCompletedOnboarding: true }), null, 'utf-8');
    }"
```

#### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#windows)  Windows

```
# Open the PowerShell terminal in Windows Terminal
# Install nodejs on Windows
# Right-click the Windows button and select "Terminal"

# Then execute the following commands in sequence
winget install OpenJS.NodeJS
Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser RemoteSigned

# Then close the terminal window and open a new one

# Install claude-code
npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code --registry=https://registry.npmmirror.com

# Initialize configuration
node --eval "
    const homeDir = os.homedir();
    const filePath = path.join(homeDir, '.claude.json');
    if (fs.existsSync(filePath)) {
        const content = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8'));
        fs.writeFileSync(filePath,JSON.stringify({ ...content, hasCompletedOnboarding: true }, 2), 'utf-8');
    } else {
        fs.writeFileSync(filePath,JSON.stringify({ hasCompletedOnboarding: true }), null, 'utf-8');
    }"
```

### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#configure-environment-variables)  Configure Environment Variables

After completing the installation of Claude Code, please set the environment variables as follows to use the `kimi-k2.5` model and start Claude.

#### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#macos-and-linux-2)  MacOS and Linux

```
# Start the high-speed kimi-k2.5 model on Linux/macOS
export ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://api.moonshot.ai/anthropic
export ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=${YOUR_MOONSHOT_API_KEY}
export ANTHROPIC_MODEL=kimi-k2.5
export ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL=kimi-k2.5
export ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=kimi-k2.5
export ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL=kimi-k2.5
export CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL=kimi-k2.5
export ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=false
claude
```

#### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#windows-2)  Windows

```
# Start the high-speed kimi-k2.5 model on Windows PowerShell
$env:ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL="https://api.moonshot.ai/anthropic";
$env:ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN="YOUR_MOONSHOT_API_KEY"
$env:ANTHROPIC_MODEL="kimi-k2.5"
$env:ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_OPUS_MODEL="kimi-k2.5"
$env:ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL="kimi-k2.5"
$env:ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL="kimi-k2.5"
$env:CLAUDE_CODE_SUBAGENT_MODEL="kimi-k2.5"
$env:ENABLE_TOOL_SEARCH=false
claude
```

## [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#using-kimi-k2-5-model-in-cline)  Using kimi k2.5 model in Cline

### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#install-cline)  Install Cline

1. Open VS Code
2. Click the Extensions icon in the left activity bar (or use the shortcut `Ctrl+Shift+X` / `Cmd+Shift+X`)
3. Enter `cline` in the search box
4. Find the `Cline` extension (usually published by Cline Team)
5. Click the `Install` button to install it
6. After installation, you may need to restart VS Code

![cline](https://mintcdn.com/moonshotai/dink2O4VJx7ks4ld/assets/pics/cline/search_cline.jpeg?fit=max&auto=format&n=dink2O4VJx7ks4ld&q=85&s=f02abbb0ab8d1751203539a06c1b7633)

### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#verify-installation)  Verify Installation

After installation, you can:

1. See the Cline icon in the left activity bar of VS Code
2. Or search for “Cline” related commands through the command palette (`Ctrl+Shift+P` / `Cmd+Shift+P`) to verify the installation was successful

### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#official-recommendation-configure-moonshot-provider-to-use-kimi-k2-5-model)  Official Recommendation: Configure Moonshot Provider to use Kimi K2.5 model

- Select ‘Moonshot’ as the API Provider
- Select ‘api.moonshot.ai’ as the Moonshot Entrypoint
- Configure the Moonshot API Key with the Key obtained from the Kimi Open Platform
- Select ‘kimi-k2.5’ as the Model
- Check ‘Disable browser tool usage’ under Browser
- Click ‘Done’ to save the configuration

![moonshot_cline](https://mintcdn.com/moonshotai/dink2O4VJx7ks4ld/assets/pics/cline/moonshot_cline_en.png?fit=max&auto=format&n=dink2O4VJx7ks4ld&q=85&s=772e07f210bd3545c2a4d5619ddcba59)

## [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#using-kimi-k2-5-model-in-opencode)  Using kimi k2.5 Model in OpenCode

### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#install-opencode)  Install OpenCode

The easiest way to install OpenCode is through the install script.

```
curl -fsSL https://opencode.ai/install | bash
```

You can also install it with npm:

```
npm install -g opencode-ai
```

### [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#configure-api-key)  Configure API key

1. Run `opencode auth login` and select **Moonshot AI**.



















```
$ opencode auth login
┌  Add credential
│
◆  Select provider
│  ● Moonshot AI
│  ...
└
```

2. Enter your Moonshot AI API key.



















```
$ opencode auth login
┌  Add credential
│
◇  Select provider
│  Moonshot AI
│
◇  Enter your API key
│  _
└
```

3. Run `opencode` to launch OpenCode.



















```
$ opencode
```











Use the `/models` command to select the _Kimi K2.5_ model.



















```
/models
```


Let’s get started. For more information, please visit [opencode.ai/docs](https://opencode.ai/docs)![opencode](https://mintcdn.com/moonshotai/dink2O4VJx7ks4ld/assets/pics/cline/opencode.jpeg?fit=max&auto=format&n=dink2O4VJx7ks4ld&q=85&s=68c313b43abd21950a0ee27f7a9c4ffb)

## [​](https://platform.kimi.ai/docs/guide/agent-support\#direct-api-calls-to-kimi-k2-5-model)  Direct API Calls to Kimi K2.5 model

- python

- curl

- node.js


```
from openai import OpenAI

client = OpenAI(
    api_key = "$MOONSHOT_API_KEY",
    base_url = "https://api.moonshot.ai/v1",
)

completion = client.chat.completions.create(
    model = "kimi-k2.5",
    messages = [\
        {"role": "system", "content": "You are Kimi, an artificial intelligence assistant provided by Moonshot AI. You are more proficient in Chinese and English conversations. You will provide users with safe, helpful, and accurate answers. At the same time, you will refuse to answer any questions involving terrorism, racial discrimination, pornography, or violence. Moonshot AI is a proper noun and cannot be translated into other languages."},\
        {"role": "user", "content": "Hello, my name is Li Lei, what is 1+1?"}\
    ]
)

print(completion.choices[0].message.content)
```

```
curl https://api.moonshot.ai/v1/chat/completions \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer $MOONSHOT_API_KEY" \
    -d '{
        "model": "kimi-k2.5",
        "messages": [\
            {"role": "system", "content": "You are Kimi, an artificial intelligence assistant provided by Moonshot AI. You are more proficient in Chinese and English conversations. You will provide users with safe, helpful, and accurate answers. At the same time, you will refuse to answer any questions involving terrorism, racial discrimination, pornography, or violence. Moonshot AI is a proper noun and cannot be translated into other languages."},\
            {"role": "user", "content": "Hello, my name is Li Lei, what is 1+1?"}\
        ]
   }'
```

```
const OpenAI = require("openai");

const client = new OpenAI({
    apiKey: "$MOONSHOT_API_KEY",
    baseURL: "https://api.moonshot.ai/v1",
});

async function main() {
    const completion = await client.chat.completions.create({
        model = "kimi-k2.5",
        messages: [\
            {"role": "system", "content": "You are Kimi, an artificial intelligence assistant provided by Moonshot AI. You are more proficient in Chinese and English conversations. You will provide users with safe, helpful, and accurate answers. At the same time, you will refuse to answer any questions involving terrorism, racial discrimination, pornography, or violence. Moonshot AI is a proper noun and cannot be translated into other languages."},\
            {"role": "user", "content": "Hello, my name is Li Lei, what is 1+1?"}\
        ]
    });
    console.log(completion.choices[0].message.content);
}

main();
```

Where $MOONSHOT\_API\_KEY needs to be replaced with the API Key you created on the platform.When running the code in the documentation using the OpenAI SDK, ensure that the Python version is at least 3.7.1, the Node.js version is at least 18, and the OpenAI SDK version is not lower than 1.0.0.

### Agent Programming

Designed for long horizon, complex reasoning tasks. Leveraging the K2 Thinking model’s reasoning capability and up to 300-step tool calling, Kimi acts as an expert-level research assistant supporting strategic research, pricing and competitive analysis, stock and crypto investing analysis.

### Legal Intelligence

Breaking resource boundaries to unlock exponential growth in core value—aligning to succeed together.

Kimi offers SOTA-level performance at a fraction of the cost. It's tremendously good at long-context tasks across the codebase, as well as the day-to-day work needed to support an always-on agent like KiloClaw.

$2.19 output | | Input Modalities | text, image | | Output Modalities | text | | Capabilities | ReasoningTool CallingTemperature ControlOpen Weights | ## Complete Setup Guide 1 ### Get Your Synthetic API Key First, you'll need to obtain an API key from Synthetic. This key allows you to access their AI models directly and pay only for what you use. 1. Visit [Synthetic's API console](https://synthetic.new/user-settings/api) 2. Sign up or log in to your account 3. Navigate to the API keys section 4. Generate a new API key (copy it immediately as some providers only show it once) 5. Save your API key in a secure password manager or encrypted note **⚠️ Important:** Keep your API key secure and never share it publicly. Store it safely as you'll need it to connect with TypingMind. 2 ### Configure TypingMind with Synthetic API Key 1. Open [TypingMind](https://typingmind.com/) in your browser 2. Click the **"Settings"** icon (gear symbol) 3. Navigate to **"Models"** section 4. Click **"Add Custom Model"** 5. Fill in the model information: **Name:**`hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5 via Synthetic` (or your preferred name) **Endpoint:**`https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1/chat/completions` **Model ID:**`hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5` **Context Length:** Enter the model's context window (e.g., 262144 for hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5) ![Synthetic Endpoint URL input field](https://www.typingmind.com/assets/guide/endpoint-url.webp)hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5https://api.synthetic.new/openai/v1/chat/completionshf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5 via Synthetichttps://www.typingmind.com/model-logo.webp262144 6. Add custom headers by clicking **"Add Custom Headers"** in the Advanced Settings section: **Authorization:**`Bearer :` **X-Title:**`typingmind.com` **HTTP-Referer:**`https://www.typingmind.com` 7. Enable **"Support Plugins (via OpenAI Functions)"** if the model supports the "functions" or "tool\_calls" parameter, or enable **"Support OpenAI Vision"** if the model supports vision. 8. Click **"Test"** to verify the configuration 9. If you see "Nice, the endpoint is working!", click **"Add Model"** 3 ### Start chatting with Kimi K2.5 Now you can start chatting with Kimi K2.5 through TypingMind: - Select Kimi K2.5 from the model dropdown menu - Start typing your message in the chat input - Enjoy faster responses and better features than the official interface - Switch between different AI models as needed ![The best frontend AI chat for hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5 via OpenRouter API Key](https://www.typingmind.com/assets/guide/chat-with-ai-demo.webp)![The best frontend AI chat for hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5 via OpenRouter API Key](https://www.typingmind.com/assets/guide/synthetic-logo.webp)hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5![The best frontend AI chat for hf:moonshotai/Kimi-K2.5 via OpenRouter API Key](https://www.typingmind.com/assets/guide/synthetic-logo.webp) 💡 Pro tips for better results: - Use specific, detailed prompts for better responses [(How to use Prompt Library)](https://docs.typingmind.com/prompts/use-prompt-library) - Create AI agents with custom instructions for repeated tasks [(How to create AI Agents)](https://docs.typingmind.com/ai-agents/ai-agents-overview) - Use plugins to extend Kimi K2.5 capabilities [(How to use plugins)](https://docs.typingmind.com/plugins/use-typingmind-plugin) ## Frequently Asked Questions Do I need a subscription to use Kimi K2.5? No! With Synthetic API, you pay only for what you use with no monthly subscription. Add credits to your Synthetic account and pay as you go. TypingMind is also a one-time purchase, not a subscription. How much will it cost to use Kimi K2.5? Kimi K2.5 costs $0.55/1M input tokens and $2.19/1M output tokens. A typical conversation might cost $0.01-0.10 depending on length. Can I use other models besides Kimi K2.5? Yes! With Synthetic API + TypingMind, you can access all Synthetic models. Switch between them instantly in TypingMind. Is my data private and secure? Yes! TypingMind stores conversations locally (web version in browser, desktop version on your device). Synthetic handles API calls securely. Check Synthetic's data policy for specifics. Can I use Kimi K2.5 for commercial projects? Yes! Check Synthetic's terms of service for specific commercial use policies. TypingMind supports commercial use. # Kimi K2.5 Pricing 2026: Plans, API Costs & Free Tier Explained

N

NxCode Team

2026-03-22•8 min read

[Share on Twitter](https://twitter.com/intent/tweet?text=Kimi%20K2.5%20Pricing%202026%3A%20Plans%2C%20API%20Costs%20%26%20Free%20Tier%20Explained&url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nxcode.io%2Fresources%2Fnews%2Fkimi-k2-5-pricing-plans-api-costs-2026)[Share on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/sharing/share-offsite/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.nxcode.io%2Fresources%2Fnews%2Fkimi-k2-5-pricing-plans-api-costs-2026)

## Kimi K2.5 Pricing in 2026: Everything You Need to Know

Kimi K2.5, released on January 27, 2026, by Moonshot AI, has rapidly become one of the most talked-about AI models thanks to its strong performance and aggressive pricing. Whether you are a developer evaluating API costs, a solo founder looking for a budget AI assistant, or a team comparing subscription plans, this guide breaks down every pricing tier, limit, and hidden cost so you can make an informed decision.

Moonshot AI positions Kimi K2.5 as a native multimodal model with visual coding capabilities, a 256K context window, and an innovative self-directed agent swarm paradigm. The model is open-source under a Modified MIT license and competes directly with GPT-5.4, Claude Sonnet 4.6, and Gemini 3.1 Pro -- but at a fraction of the cost.

* * *

## Quick Pricing Overview

Here is a summary of every Kimi K2.5 pricing tier available in March 2026:

| Plan | Price | What You Get |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Free (Chat)** | $0 | Daily message limit (30-50 messages/day), K2.5 model access, 256K context |
| **Pro / Moderato (Chat)** | ~$19/month (or ~49 CNY/month in China) | Higher daily limits, priority access, advanced features |
| **API Free Tier** | $0 (requires $1 initial recharge) | Low rate limits (3 RPM), pay-per-token usage |
| **API Paid Tiers** | Pay-as-you-go | $0.60/M input tokens, $2.50-$3.00/M output tokens, higher rate limits based on recharge amount |
| **API via OpenRouter** | Pay-as-you-go | $0.45/M input tokens, $2.20/M output tokens |
| **API via Together AI** | Pay-as-you-go | $0.50/M input tokens, $2.80/M output tokens |

Prices are current as of March 2026. Moonshot AI adjusts rates periodically, so always verify on [platform.moonshot.ai](https://platform.moonshot.ai/) before committing.

* * *

### Describe what you want — NxCode builds it for you.

Turn your idea into a working app — no coding required.

Start Free

## Free Tier: What You Get for $0

Kimi offers a genuinely usable free tier through its chat interface at [kimi.com](https://www.kimi.com/). Here is what is included:

**Included in the free plan:**

- Access to Kimi K2.5, Moonshot AI's flagship model
- 256K token context window (double what most competitors offer for free)
- Multimodal input (text and images)
- Web search integration
- File upload and analysis
- Daily message limit of approximately 30 to 50 messages

**Free tier limitations:**

- Daily message caps that reset every 24 hours
- No priority access during peak usage periods
- Limited access to advanced agent and automation features
- The interface is primarily in Chinese, though the model understands English well

For casual users, researchers, or anyone wanting to test Kimi K2.5 before committing, the free tier is generous. The 256K context window alone sets it apart from most free AI chatbot offerings.

* * *

## Pro Plan: Premium Chat Access

Moonshot AI offers paid subscription plans for users who need more than the free tier provides. Pricing varies by region:

**China pricing:**

- Approximately 49 CNY/month (~$7-8 USD) for the basic paid membership
- Annual discounts available

**International pricing:**

- Moderato plan at approximately $19/month with auto-renewal
- Higher-tier plans available at $49/month for power users

**What Pro unlocks:**

- Significantly higher daily message limits
- Priority model access with faster response times
- Advanced agent and workflow features
- Kimi Code membership benefits for developers
- Enhanced file processing capabilities

**Who should get Pro:**

- Daily power users who hit the free message cap regularly
- Professionals using Kimi for work tasks in Chinese or English
- Developers who prefer the chat interface over the API for prototyping

Note that the Pro subscription and the API are billed separately. If you are building applications, the API is almost certainly the better value. The Pro plan is designed for individual users who interact with Kimi through its web or mobile interfaces.

* * *

## API Pricing: Per-Token Costs by Model

The Kimi API is where Moonshot AI's pricing truly stands out. The API is fully compatible with OpenAI's SDK, meaning you can use it as a drop-in replacement for the OpenAI API by changing the endpoint to `api.moonshot.ai/v1`.

### Direct API Pricing (platform.moonshot.ai)

| Model | Input (per 1M tokens) | Output (per 1M tokens) | Context Length |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Kimi K2.5** | $0.60 | $2.50 - $3.00 | 256K |
| **Kimi K2** | $0.60 | $2.50 | 128K |

### Third-Party Provider Pricing

You can also access Kimi K2.5 through third-party inference providers, often at lower prices:

| Provider | Input (per 1M tokens) | Output (per 1M tokens) | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **OpenRouter** | $0.45 | $2.20 | Lowest blended price among major providers |
| **Together AI** | $0.50 | $2.80 | Good balance of price and reliability |
| **DeepInfra** | ~$0.90 blended | -- | Competitive blended rate |
| **Nebius** | ~$1.00 blended | -- | Fast inference option |

### Context Caching Discount

One of the most developer-friendly features of the Kimi API is automatic context caching. When you send repeated or overlapping prompts, the system automatically caches context and reduces your input costs by up to **75%**. No configuration is required -- it happens transparently. This can dramatically reduce costs for applications that maintain conversation history or process similar documents repeatedly.

### Getting Started with the API

To start using the Kimi API, you need to:

1. Sign up at [platform.moonshot.ai](https://platform.moonshot.ai/)
2. Recharge at least $1 to activate your account
3. When your cumulative recharge reaches $5, you receive a $5 voucher (vouchers do not count toward recharge tier thresholds)

* * *

## API Rate Limits by Recharge Tier

Moonshot AI ties your API rate limits to how much you have recharged cumulatively. Here are the tiers:

| Recharge Tier | Cumulative Amount | Concurrency | RPM (Requests/Min) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Starter** | $1 - $9 | Low | ~3 RPM |
| **Tier 1** | $10+ | 50 | 200 RPM |
| **Tier 2** | $50+ | Higher | Higher RPM |
| **Tier 3** | $500+ | Higher | Higher RPM |
| **Tier 5** | $3,000+ | 1,000 | 10,000 RPM |

For usage beyond Tier 5, contact Moonshot AI at [sales@moonshot.cn](mailto:sales@moonshot.cn) to negotiate custom limits.

* * *

## Pricing Comparison: Kimi K2.5 vs ChatGPT vs Claude vs Gemini

Here is how Kimi K2.5 stacks up against competing AI models on both subscription and API pricing in March 2026:

### Subscription / Chat Plan Comparison

| Service | Free Tier | Pro/Paid Plan | Context Window (Free) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Kimi K2.5** | 30-50 msgs/day | ~$8-19/month | 256K tokens |
| **ChatGPT (GPT-5.4)** | Limited msgs/day | $20/month (Plus) | 128K tokens |
| **Claude (Sonnet 4.6)** | Limited msgs/day | $20/month (Pro) | 200K tokens |
| **Gemini (3.1 Pro)** | Limited msgs/day | $20/month (Advanced) | 1M tokens |

### API Token Pricing Comparison

| Model | Input (per 1M tokens) | Output (per 1M tokens) | Ratio vs Kimi |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **Kimi K2.5** | $0.60 | $2.50 | 1x (baseline) |
| **GPT-5.4** | $2.50 - $10.00 | $10.00 - $30.00 | 4-17x more expensive |
| **Claude Sonnet 4.6** | $3.00 | $15.00 | 5-6x more expensive |
| **Claude Opus 4.6** | $15.00 | $75.00 | 25-30x more expensive |
| **Gemini 3.1 Pro** | $1.25 | $5.00 | 2x more expensive |
| **DeepSeek V4** | $0.27 | $1.10 | ~0.5x (cheaper) |

**Key takeaway:** Kimi K2.5 is one of the most cost-effective frontier-quality models available. Only DeepSeek V4 undercuts it on raw token pricing, but Kimi K2.5 holds advantages in multimodal capabilities and the agent swarm paradigm.

* * *

## Is Kimi K2.5 Worth the Price?

The value proposition depends entirely on your use case. Here is an honest assessment:

**Where Kimi K2.5 delivers outstanding value:**

- API-heavy applications where token costs dominate your bill
- Projects requiring long context windows (256K tokens included at no extra cost)
- Multimodal workflows involving visual coding or image analysis
- Applications where OpenAI SDK compatibility makes migration trivial
- Developers who want open-source model weights for self-hosting

**Where Kimi K2.5 may fall short:**

- The chat interface is Chinese-first, which can be a barrier for non-Chinese speakers
- Ecosystem maturity (plugins, integrations, community resources) lags behind OpenAI and Anthropic
- Enterprise support and SLA guarantees are less established than Western competitors
- English-language documentation is improving but still behind competitors

**Cost savings example:** A SaaS application processing 100 million tokens per month would pay approximately $310 with Kimi K2.5 ($60 input + $250 output) versus $4,000+ with GPT-5.4. That is a savings of over $3,600 per month, or $43,000 per year.

* * *

## Who Should Use Kimi K2.5?

**Budget-conscious startups and indie developers.** If API costs are a significant line item in your budget, Kimi K2.5 offers frontier-level performance at a fraction of what OpenAI or Anthropic charge. The OpenAI-compatible API means migration is straightforward.

**Teams building for the Chinese market.** Kimi K2.5 has native Chinese language understanding that outperforms any Western model. If your product serves Chinese-speaking users, Kimi is the natural choice.

**Developers who need long context.** The 256K context window is available at no premium. Processing long documents, codebases, or maintaining extended conversations is cheaper with Kimi than with competitors that charge extra for extended context.

**Open-source advocates.** With a Modified MIT license, you can inspect, modify, and self-host Kimi K2.5. This gives you full control over your AI stack -- something you cannot get with GPT-5.4 or Claude.

**Who should look elsewhere:**

- Teams that need battle-tested enterprise SLAs and compliance certifications
- Non-technical users who want the most polished English-language chat experience
- Organizations locked into the OpenAI or Anthropic ecosystem with significant tooling dependencies

* * *

## How to Sign Up for Kimi K2.5

### For the Chat Interface (Free or Pro)

1. Visit [kimi.com](https://www.kimi.com/)
2. Create an account using a phone number or email
3. Start chatting immediately on the free tier
4. Upgrade to Pro through the membership/pricing page if needed

### For the API

1. Go to [platform.moonshot.ai](https://platform.moonshot.ai/)
2. Register a developer account
3. Recharge a minimum of $1 to activate API access
4. Generate your API key from the dashboard
5. Use the endpoint `https://api.moonshot.ai/v1` with the OpenAI SDK -- just swap your base URL and API key

### Via Third-Party Providers

If you want to avoid managing a separate account with Moonshot AI, you can access Kimi K2.5 through:

- [OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai/moonshotai/kimi-k2.5) \-\- lowest third-party price
- [Together AI](https://www.together.ai/models/kimi-k2-5) \-\- strong uptime and documentation
- DeepInfra, Nebius, and other inference providers

* * *

## The Bottom Line

Kimi K2.5 is the price-performance leader among frontier AI models in early 2026. At $0.60 per million input tokens and $2.50 per million output tokens, it undercuts GPT-5.4 by 4-17x and Claude Sonnet 4.6 by 5-6x while delivering competitive benchmark results.

The free chat tier is generous enough for casual and research use. The Pro subscription at $8-19/month (depending on region) is significantly cheaper than the $20/month charged by ChatGPT Plus, Claude Pro, and Gemini Advanced. And the API pricing, especially with automatic 75% context caching discounts, makes it one of the most economical choices for production applications.

The main trade-offs are a Chinese-first interface, a younger ecosystem, and less established enterprise support compared to OpenAI and Anthropic. But if cost efficiency is your priority -- and it should be for most startups -- Kimi K2.5 deserves serious consideration.

Start with the free tier to evaluate quality, then move to the API if you are building products. Your infrastructure budget will thank you.

import { Agent } from "@voltagent/core";

# Providers and Models [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#providers-and-models)

Kimi Code CLI supports multiple LLM platforms, which can be configured via configuration files or the `/login` command.

## Platform selection [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#platform-selection)

The easiest way to configure is to run the `/login` command (alias `/setup`) in shell mode and follow the wizard to select platform and model:

1. Select an API platform
2. Enter your API key
3. Select a model from the available list

After configuration, Kimi Code CLI will automatically save settings to `~/.kimi/config.toml` and reload.

`/login` currently supports the following platforms:

| Platform | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Kimi Code | Kimi Code platform, supports search and fetch services |
| Moonshot AI Open Platform (moonshot.cn) | China region API endpoint |
| Moonshot AI Open Platform (moonshot.ai) | Global region API endpoint |

For other platforms, please manually edit the configuration file.

## Provider types [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#provider-types)

The `type` field in `providers` configuration specifies the API provider type. Different types use different API protocols and client implementations.

| Type | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `kimi` | Kimi API |
| `openai_legacy` | OpenAI Chat Completions API |
| `openai_responses` | OpenAI Responses API |
| `anthropic` | Anthropic Claude API |
| `gemini` | Google Gemini API |
| `vertexai` | Google Vertex AI |

All provider types support adding custom HTTP headers via the `custom_headers` field. See [Configuration files](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/config-files.html) for details.

### `kimi` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#kimi)

For connecting to Kimi API, including Kimi Code and Moonshot AI Open Platform.

toml

```
[providers.kimi-for-coding]
type = "kimi"
base_url = "https://api.kimi.com/coding/v1"
api_key = "sk-xxx"
```

### `openai_legacy` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#openai-legacy)

For platforms compatible with OpenAI Chat Completions API, including the official OpenAI API and various compatible services.

toml

```
[providers.openai]
type = "openai_legacy"
base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
api_key = "sk-xxx"
```

### `openai_responses` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#openai-responses)

For OpenAI Responses API (newer API format).

toml

```
[providers.openai-responses]
type = "openai_responses"
base_url = "https://api.openai.com/v1"
api_key = "sk-xxx"
```

### `anthropic` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#anthropic)

For connecting to Anthropic Claude API.

toml

```
[providers.anthropic]
type = "anthropic"
base_url = "https://api.anthropic.com"
api_key = "sk-ant-xxx"
```

### `gemini` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#gemini)

For connecting to Google Gemini API.

toml

```
[providers.gemini]
type = "gemini"
base_url = "https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com"
api_key = "xxx"
```

### `vertexai` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#vertexai)

For connecting to Google Vertex AI. Requires setting necessary environment variables via the `env` field.

toml

```
[providers.vertexai]
type = "vertexai"
base_url = "https://xxx-aiplatform.googleapis.com"
api_key = ""
env = { GOOGLE_CLOUD_PROJECT = "your-project-id" }
```

## Model capabilities [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#model-capabilities)

The `capabilities` field in model configuration declares the capabilities supported by the model. This affects feature availability in Kimi Code CLI.

| Capability | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `thinking` | Supports thinking mode (deep reasoning), can be toggled |
| `always_thinking` | Always uses thinking mode (cannot be disabled) |
| `image_in` | Supports image input |
| `video_in` | Supports video input |

toml

```
[models.gemini-3-pro-preview]
provider = "gemini"
model = "gemini-3-pro-preview"
max_context_size = 262144
capabilities = ["thinking", "image_in"]
```

### `thinking` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#thinking)

Declares that the model supports thinking mode. When enabled, the model performs deeper reasoning before answering, suitable for complex problems. In shell mode, you can use the `/model` command to switch models and thinking mode, or control it at startup with `--thinking` / `--no-thinking` flags.

### `always_thinking` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#always-thinking)

Indicates the model always uses thinking mode and cannot be disabled. For example, models with "thinking" in their name like `kimi-k2-thinking-turbo` typically have this capability. When using such models, the `/model` command won't prompt for thinking mode toggle.

### `image_in` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#image-in)

When image input capability is enabled, you can paste images in conversations (`Ctrl-V`).

### `video_in` [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#video-in)

When video input capability is enabled, you can send video content in conversations.

## Search and fetch services [​](https://moonshotai.github.io/kimi-cli/en/configuration/providers.html\#search-and-fetch-services)

The `SearchWeb` and `FetchURL` tools depend on external services, currently only provided by the Kimi Code platform.

When selecting the Kimi Code platform using `/login`, search and fetch services are automatically configured.

| Service | Corresponding tool | Behavior when not configured |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `moonshot_search` | `SearchWeb` | Tool unavailable |
| `moonshot_fetch` | `FetchURL` | Falls back to local fetching |

When using other platforms, the `FetchURL` tool is still available but will fall back to local fetching.
